title: Editor and terminal workflow deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Editor and terminal workflow; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Editor and terminal workflow: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? Typing code in an editor automatically runs it, and every terminal starts in the folder containing the visible file.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Save intentionally, inspect the working directory and command, run the declared tool, and read its output or diagnostic as a separate workflow step.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Save intentionally, inspect the working directory and command, run the declared tool, and read its output or diagnostic as a separate workflow step.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Editor and terminal workflow instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: Save intentionally, inspect the working directory and command, run the declared tool, and read its output or diagnostic as a separate workflow step.
Typing code in an editor automatically runs it, and every terminal starts in the folder containing the visible file.
